repos.name: Zentrix Pulse
issues: Metered API key hardcoded in .env.local, committed to git history
.env and .env.local not properly gitignored, secrets in git history
CORS set to wildcard '*' in Supabase edge functions shared config
Supabase publishable keys and project URL committed in .env file
Early migration had permissive RLS policies (mitigated by later migration)

repos.name: DietForge
issues: .env credentials (Supabase keys) exposed in git history, not yet scrubbed
5 high-severity npm vulnerabilities (serialize-javascript RCE, xlsx prototype pollution)
Potential XSS in ForgePediaArticle.tsx custom markdown renderer before DOMPurify pass
No Content-Security-Policy headers configured in application source
Console.error calls (~40+) may leak sensitive stack traces in production

repos.name: Zentrix Insights
issues: Could not complete scan

repos.name: Zentrix Mail
issues: Supabase API keys exposed in git history, need rotation and history rewrite
.env file with credentials exists in working directory despite .gitignore
Wildcard CORS (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) on all 15 Supabase edge functions
CSP includes 'unsafe-eval' in script-src, weakening XSS protection
Unauthenticated track-email-open endpoint exposes email open tracking

repos.name: Lineage Vault
issues: .env with Supabase credentials committed to git history (commit 6f408e2)
15 npm vulnerabilities (8 high): react-router XSS, rollup path traversal, yaml DoS
/design route missing PageGuard authentication check in App.tsx
innerHTML usage in BucketListMap.tsx without sanitization (low XSS risk)
63+ files with console.error logging that could leak app internals in production

repos.name: Zentrix Cyber
issues: Supabase credentials committed to repo in .env file (critical secret exposure)
Users can update their own role via RLS policy enabling privilege escalation
CORS set to wildcard Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on all edge functions
JWT verification disabled for threat-ingestion and automation-engine functions
Server-side functions log user IDs, threat details, and full error stacks

repos.name: Zentrix Projects
issues: Supabase edge functions (automation-engine, ai-chat) lack auth checks on endpoints
SSRF risk: automation-engine fetches user-controlled URLs without validation
.env with Supabase credentials committed to repo despite being in .gitignore
dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage in chart.tsx without strict sanitization
Error objects logged to console and raw error.message shown to users in toasts

repos.name: Decision Command
issues: Supabase API key and JWT token committed in .env file (critical secret exposure)
Auth/admin checks are client-side only; no server-side route authorization
Client-side rate limiting on login easily bypassed via localStorage clearing
console.error not suppressed in production, may leak auth error details
dangerouslySetInnerHTML used in chart.tsx without strict input validation

repos.name: Improvements Dashboard
issues: .env contains real Slack token (xoxb-*) and API key on disk, risk of accidental commit
Auth bypassed when API_KEY env var is unset: `if (!API_KEY || key === API_KEY)` passes all
CORS allows all origins (`origin: '*'`), any site can call the action endpoint
innerHTML used with external JSON data (entries, summaries) without sanitization — XSS risk
Error internals leaked to clients via `e.message` in 500 responses

repos.name: WiseVAs
issues: Hardcoded Supabase anon key as fallback in portal-clients.ts (public but in git)
dangerouslySetInnerHTML in chart.tsx (mitigated: CSS-only, no user input)
Potential open redirect via action_url in ContactUs.tsx notifications handler
Sentry session replay captures unmasked text (maskAllText: false)
Server-side logs may leak auth context (password update errors in edge functions)

repos.name: Zentrix CRM
issues: .env with Supabase credentials exposed in git history (committed before .gitignore fix)
CORS set to Allow-Origin: * on all 43+ Supabase Edge Functions enables CSRF
ingest-external-lead compares API keys in plaintext, not hashed like other functions
Any tenant member can create webhook API keys, no role-based restriction
Platform keys accept events for any tenant with minimal access controls

repos.name: Zentrix Creative
issues: CRITICAL: edit-concept-image function has no authentication check at all
CRITICAL: extract-brand-colors has broken authorization (user_id field doesn't exist)
HIGH: .env file with Supabase credentials committed to git history
MEDIUM: dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage and token storage in localStorage (XSS risk)
MEDIUM: Sensitive user data logged to console in multiple edge functions

repos.name: Zentrix OS
issues: .env.local with Supabase JWT tokens committed to git repo
Hardcoded Bearer tokens in 8+ SQL migration files and docs
Build artifacts (dist/) contain embedded Supabase credentials
Stack traces exposed to end users in error boundary components
6 npm audit vulnerabilities including 1 high-severity (picomatch ReDoS)

repos.name: Thoughtline
issues: 6 Supabase edge functions lack JWT auth, allowing unauthorized access to user data
ai-chat and analyze-tag accept client-provided userId without verification (IDOR)
User thought content and messages logged via console.log in edge functions
dangerouslySetInnerHTML used in chart.tsx (safe context, but worth noting)
.env with Supabase anon key exists locally (not git-tracked, but no .env.example)

Supabase JWT anon key exposed in git history (needs rotation if repo was public)
git rm --cached .env was never completed; .env still in git index
No pre-commit hook or secret scanning to prevent future credential leaks
CORS fallback hardcoded to zentrixmarketing.com if ALLOWED_ORIGIN env var unset
